popularStory.headline: Henry IV (German: Heinrich IV; 11 November 1050-7 August 1106)
popularStory.description: Henry IV (German: Heinrich IV; 11 November 1050-7 August 1106) ascended to King of the Germans in 1056. From 1084 until his forced abdication in 1105, he was also referred to as the King of the Romans …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser於1050年11月11日出生在德國尼德薩辛布勞恩史維時，他的父親Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser33歲，母親Agnès de Poitiers25歲。他於1066年7月13日與Berta di Savoia結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1106年8月7日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇利艾享年55歲，埋葬在德國海藍-普法爾茲斯培也斯培也。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser于1050年11月11日出生在德国尼德薩辛布勞恩史維时，他的父亲Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser33岁，母亲Agnès de Poitiers25岁。他于1066年7月13日与Berta di Savoia结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1106年8月7日在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇利艾享年55岁，埋葬在德国海藍-普法爾茲斯培也斯培也。

nameMeanings.name: Salier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for an extractor or seller of salt (a precious commodity in medieval times), from Middle English salter(e) ‘producer or seller of salt’. There may have been some post-medieval confusion with Souter ; see also Saltman .

English: occupational name from Middle English sautreour ‘minstrel, player of the psaltery (a stringed musical instrument)’ (Old French sautere ‘psaltery’, Latin psalterium, Greek psaltērion, from psallein ‘to sound’). Compare Rutter .
German: northern variant of   Salzer  .

variants.name: Salois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Fabien, Normand.
French: unexplained. This surname is very rare in France. Compare   Salter  ,   Saltus  , and   Salway  .
History: Claude Salois from Lille in Nord, France, married Anne Mabille in Quebec City, QC, c. 1666.

variants.name: Salt
meanings: English: habitational name from Salt (Staffordshire), from Old English selte ‘salt pit’. Occasionally the name may be a nickname for a producer or seller of salt, from Middle English salt ‘salt’ (compare Salter ), but evidence is lacking.
Native American (Navajo): from a translation into English of the name of the Navajo clan Áshįįhi ‘Salt People’.

variants.name: Saulter
meanings: English: variant of   Salter  .

variants.name: Saltman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name for a producer or seller of salt, from Middle English salt ‘salt’ + -man. See also Salter .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Salzmann or Jewish (Ashkenazic) Saltsman , or of their rare German cognate Saltmann.

variants.name: Souter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English souter(e), suter(e) ‘shoemaker, cobbler’ (Old English sūtere, from Latin sūtor, an agent derivative of suere ‘to sew’). In early Modern English, pronunciation of the name would regularly develop from /su:ter/ to /sautə(r)/ (rhyming with outer), or to /sutə(r)/, as in Sutter . In Scots, however, it remained unchanged, with the spelling -ou- representing /u/. In parts of southern England it seems also to have remained as /u:/; see Suter (2).
Americanized form of German   Sauter  , a cognate of 1 above.
Flemish (De Souter): occupational name for a producer or seller of salt, from an agent derivative of Middle Dutch sout ‘salt’.

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power’. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: IV.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser was born on 11 November 1050, in Brunswick, Lower Saxony, Germany, his father, Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser, was 33 and his mother, Agnès de Poitiers, was 25. He married Berta di Savoia on 13 July 1066. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 7 August 1106, in Liège, Holy Roman Empire, at the age of 55, and was buried in Speyer, Speyer, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser est né le 11 novembre 1050, à Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, son père, Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Agnès de Poitiers, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Berta di Savoia le 13 juillet 1066. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 7 août 1106, à Liège, Saint-Empire romain, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser am 11. November 1050 in Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser 33 und seine Mutter Agnès de Poitiers 25. Er hat am 13. Juli 1066 Berta di Savoia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 7. August 1106 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Lüttich, Heiliges Römisches Reich und wurde in Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser è nato il il 11 novembre 1050 nel Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Germania, il suo padre, Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Agnès de Poitiers, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Berta di Savoia il 13 luglio 1066. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 7 agosto 1106, nel Liegi, Heiliges Römisches Reich, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiserが1050年11月11日にドイツニーダーサチセンブラウンスチウエイグで生まれた時，父Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiserは33歳，母Agnès de Poitiersは25歳であった。1066年7月13日にBerta di Savoiaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1106年8月7日にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチリeッジで55歳で死去し，ドイツルヘインランド-プファルズスペヤースペヤーにて埋葬された。

ko.Bad Harzburg, Goslar, Hanover, Prussia,: 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 해노버 고슬라 바드 하르즈버그
ko.Bad Harzburg, Goslar, Lower Saxony, Germ: 독일 니더작센 랜드크레이스 고슬라 바드 하르즈버그
독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 맹즈-빙 잉하임 아 리인
ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser(이)가 1050년 11월 11일 독일 니더작센 브라슈위그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnès de Poitiers(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1066년 7월 13일 Berta di Savoia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1106년 8월 7일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 리e지에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 스피어 스피어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser nasceu em 11 de novembro de 1050, em Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, seu pai, Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Agnès de Poitiers, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Berta di Savoia em 13 de julho de 1066. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de agosto de 1106, em Liège, Heiliges Römisches Reich, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser родился 11 ноября 1050 г. в местности браунщвиг, ниедирсачсен, Германия, его отцу по имени Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Agnès de Poitiers, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Berta di Savoia 13 июля 1066 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 7 августа 1106 г. в местности лиeдж, хилигесь рoмищесь раич в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности спиир, спиир, раинланд-пфалз, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heinrich IV. Salier Römischer Kaiser nació el 11 de noviembre de 1050, en Braunschweig, Niedersachsen, Alemania, su papá, Heinrich III. Salier Römisch-Deutscher Kaiser, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Agnès de Poitiers, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Berta di Savoia el 13 de julio de 1066. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 7 de agosto de 1106, en Lieja, Heiliges Römisches Reich, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Speyer, Speyer, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania.
